narrative: In 1993, Macao Special Administrative Region experienced a rare storm that passed without any recorded fatalities or damage.
This anomaly is a testament to the region's preparedness and the power of nature's unpredictability.
The storm serves as a reminder of the importance of vigilance and resilience in the face of natural disasters.

This year, the Macao Special Administrative Region was struck by a storm that left a lasting impact. The disaster caused 10 fatalities, and estimated damages of $1,817,228,000 USD. Despite the devastation, the resilience of the community shone through as they worked together to rebuild and recover.
In 2025, China's Macao Special Administrative Region faced a storm, a tempestuous test of resilience.
Despite the turbulent weather, the region was spared from any loss of life, a merciful reprieve.
The financial toll was also negligible, with zero estimated damage, a testament to the region's preparedness and good fortune.
